title: Domain
description: Describes a bounded business domain that spans one or more repositories. Produced during onboarding through manual knowledge capture with the domain team. Stored in the state store and referenced by the orchestration engine during triage and planning.

description.description: What this domain does, its boundaries, and what it does not own.

repositories.description: The repositories that belong to this domain.

team.description: Primary team that owns this domain (e.g., GitHub team slug or org team name).

escalation_contacts.description: People or groups to escalate to when the agent cannot proceed.

domain_experts.description: People with deep tribal knowledge of this domain. Captured during onboarding.

cross_repo_dependencies.description: Explicit dependencies between repositories in this domain. Used during planning to determine step ordering for multi-repo tasks.

tribal_knowledge.description: Captured domain knowledge that is not in documentation or code. This is the most valuable output of onboarding — conventions, gotchas, implicit rules that only the team knows.

release_process.description: How releases happen across this domain's repos.

testing_philosophy.description: Domain-level testing approach (e.g., 'integration tests across services required before merge').

deployment_order.description: When deploying changes across repos, this is the safe order. References repo IDs from the repositories array.

notes.description: Any other domain-wide conventions that don't fit the above fields.

name.description: Name of the external dependency (e.g., 'Stripe API', 'Auth0', 'PostgreSQL').

notes.description: Anything the agent should know about this dependency.

external_dependencies.description: External systems this domain depends on that are outside the org's control.

onboarded_at.description: When this domain was onboarded to the framework.

last_reviewed_at.description: When the domain contract was last reviewed by the team for accuracy.

role.description: What role this repo plays in the domain (e.g., 'primary API', 'shared library', 'worker', 'frontend').

notes.description: Anything specific about this repo the agent should know.

from_repo.description: The repo that depends on another.

topic.description: Short label for the knowledge area (e.g., 'payment retries', 'auth token rotation').

content.description: The actual knowledge — what the team knows that isn't written down.

source.description: Who provided this knowledge (e.g., 'Jane Doe, staff engineer').
